blocks.text: Since this 4WD does not have a mechanical mechanism to switch to 2WD, when servicing the CVT, there are some items that require special attention specific to 4WD vehicles that are different from those of 2WD vehicles. If a 4WD vehicle is driven with the engine running by lifting the front wheels while the rear wheels are still on the ground, the power will be transmitted to the rear wheels, causing the vehicle to fly off the road, which is dangerous. Therefore, when performing inspections that require the wheels to rotate, such as hydraulic pressure measurements, be sure to lift up all four wheels so that they can rotate freely before performing the inspections.
When using a speedometer tester, a simple free roller shall be used on the rear wheels and the following precautions shall be taken during the test.

items.text: Inspection and measurement using a chassis dynamometer is not allowed.
To prevent danger, be sure to set and secure the anti-sway ropes on the vehicle.
The free roller should be set parallel to the speedometer tester roller.
Never accelerate, brake, or steer the vehicle suddenly, as it is dangerous and may cause the vehicle to roll over.
The maximum speed shall be 50 km/h or less.
Continuous use shall be limited to 3 minutes or less.

blocks.text: Set the free rollers to match the wheelbase and tread of the vehicle.

Place the front wheels of the vehicle on the speedometer tester and the rear wheels on the free rollers. Ensure that the center of the front and rear wheels are centered on the speedometer tester and free roller.
Secure the vehicle using towing hooks and tie-down hooks to prevent lateral swing.
Start the engine and turn the select lever to the D position.
Start with the accelerator pedal depressed slowly, and then gradually increase the speed to take the measurement.
After the measurement, the vehicle is gradually decelerated to a stop by gentle brake operation.
